description: You are preparing a ruggedized laptop for a SOTA (Summits on the Air) activation on Mount Whitney. Configure GPredict for this offline, high-glare environment by creating a ground station named 'Mt_Whitney' (36.5786 N, 118.2920 W, 4421 m) and a tracking module named 'SOTA_FM' containing satellites SO-50 (NORAD 27607), ISS (ZARYA) (NORAD 25544), and AO-27 (NORAD 22825). The 'SOTA_FM' module must be bound to the 'Mt_Whitney' ground station. Additionally, set the map image to 'mblue.png' to mitigate intense sunlight glare and enable automatic background TLE data updates to ensure orbital data is cached. Login: username 'ga', password 'password123'. GPredict is already open.

natural_language.prompt: I'm heading up Mount Whitney for a SOTA activation and need my GPredict set up for the peak. First, make a ground station called 'Mt_Whitney' at 36.5786 North, 118.2920 West, and 4421 meters high. Then, make a tracking module called 'SOTA_FM' and add the main FM sats: SO-50 (27607), ISS (25544), and AO-27 (22825). Make sure you specifically set this module to use the Mt_Whitney ground station. Also, the sun glare up there is brutal, so go into preferences and change the map image to 'mblue.png' so I can actually read it. Finally, since I'll only have Wi-Fi at the trailhead, turn on 'Update TLE data automatically in the background' in the TLE Update preferences so it caches orbital data automatically when I turn on the laptop.
